subject: internal modeling APIs
old: internal modeling APIs (layer declarations, mask/cache construction, hybrid-attention handling)
new: standardized layer declarations, mask/cache construction, and hybrid-attention handling
truth: Internal modeling APIs for layer declarations, mask/cache construction, and hybrid-attention handling have been standardized; code relying on the old internal APIs must be updated.
quote: A broad set of modeling changes have been made to standardize layer declarations, mask/cache construction, and hybrid-attention handling, making many models cleanly exportable (ONNX, torch.export, ExecuTorch) and fullgraph-compilable — users relying on internal modeling APIs may need to update their code accordingly.

from_fact: internal modeling apis (layer declarations, mask/cache construction, hybrid-attention handling)
standardized layer declarations, mask/cache construction, and hybrid-attention handling

subject: Gemma 3/4 attention masking for image tokens
old: bidirectional attention masking for image tokens crossing sliding window boundaries in local layers
new: attention masking correctly respects sliding window boundaries in local layers
truth: Gemma 3/4 attention masking for image tokens now correctly respects sliding window boundaries in local layers, changing model behavior and potentially breaking reproducibility of previous results.
quote: Attention masking for image tokens in Gemma 3/4 models has been fixed to correctly respect sliding window boundaries in local layers, which changes model behavior and may affect reproducibility of previous results.

from_fact: bidirectional attention masking for image tokens crossing sliding window boundaries in local layers
attention masking correctly respects sliding window boundaries in local layers

subject: Kernels integration model-type repositories
old: model-type repositories accepted by the kernels interface
new: updated kernel repository format
truth: The Kernels integration no longer accepts model-type repositories; users must migrate to the updated kernel repository format.
quote: The Kernels integration has been synced to the latest version, which includes a breaking change where model-type repositories are no longer accepted by the kernels interface — users must migrate to the updated kernel repository format as shown in the updated tests.

from_fact: model-type repositories accepted by the kernels interface

subject: SDPA math-kernel fallback for GQA models
old: SDPA math-kernel fallback silently fell back to O(S²) memory for GQA models with head_dim > 256
new: SDPA math-kernel fallback no longer silently materializes O(S²) memory for GQA models with head_dim > 256
truth: GQA models with key/value head_dim > 256 no longer silently fall back to O(S²) memory materialization in the SDPA math-kernel.
quote: Fix silent SDPA math-kernel fallback for GQA when key/value head_dim > 256 or differ (#46960)

from_fact: sdpa math-kernel fallback silently fell back to o(s²) memory for gqa models with head_dim > 256
sdpa math-kernel fallback no longer silently materializes o(s²) memory for gqa models with head_dim > 256

subject: use_kernels=True kernel mapping application
old: use_kernels=True failed to apply kernel mappings (regression)
new: use_kernels=True now properly applies kernel mappings
truth: Setting use_kernels=True now correctly applies kernel mappings instead of silently ignoring them.
quote: [`Kernels`] Trigger proper kernelization on `use_kernels=True` (#46755)

from_fact: use_kernels=true failed to apply kernel mappings (regression)
use_kernels=true now properly applies kernel mappings

subject: default kernel mapping import behavior
old: default kernel mapping was eagerly built, causing import failures with incompatible kernel versions
new: default kernel mapping is now lazily built to decouple from normal transformers usage
truth: Default kernel mapping is now lazily loaded to prevent import failures when incompatible kernel versions are installed.
quote: Lazily build the default kernel mapping to decouple `kernels` from normal transformers usage (#46681)

from_fact: default kernel mapping was eagerly built, causing import failures with incompatible kernel versions
default kernel mapping is now lazily built to decouple from normal transformers usage

subject: assisted generation for LFM2 and LFM2-MoE
old: assisted generation was allowed for LFM2 and LFM2-MoE (stateful models)
new: assisted generation is now rejected for LFM2 and LFM2-MoE (set _is_stateful)
truth: Assisted generation is now rejected for LFM2 and LFM2-MoE because they are stateful models.
quote: Reject assisted generation for LFM2 and LFM2-MoE (set _is_stateful) (#46937)

from_fact: assisted generation was allowed for lfm2 and lfm2-moe (stateful models)
assisted generation is now rejected for lfm2 and lfm2-moe (set _is_stateful)

subject: continuous batching output mutation
old: continuous batching mutated request state during generation output snapshotting
new: continuous batching now snapshots generation outputs without mutating request state
truth: Continuous batching no longer mutates request state when snapshotting generation outputs.
quote: [Continuous Batching] Snapshot generation outputs without mutating request state (#46670)

from_fact: continuous batching mutated request state during generation output snapshotting
continuous batching now snapshots generation outputs without mutating request state

subject: Cache API getters/properties
old: old cache getters/properties
new: consolidated `get_max_length` method
truth: Stop using old cache getters/properties; use the consolidated `get_max_length` method instead.
quote: Rework some old cache getters/properties (#46862) by @Cyrilvallez in #46862

subject: FP8 quantization (ue8m0 format)
old: FP8 quantization that did not round block scales before quantizing weights
new: FP8 quantization now rounds block scales before quantizing weights
truth: FP8 quantization now rounds block scales before quantizing weights, so dequantization produces correct values for ue8m0 format; previously dequantized values were incorrect.
quote: Round the ue8m0 FP8 scale before quantizing so dequant matches the stored inverse (#46763) by @Incheonkirin in #46763

from_fact: fp8 quantization that did not round block scales before quantizing weights
fp8 quantization now rounds block scales before quantizing weights

subject: Gemma4 vision/audio embedders with BitsAndBytes quantization
old: inputs incorrectly cast to integer storage dtypes (uint8/int8)
new: inputs cast to actual compute dtype
truth: Gemma4's vision and audio multimodal embedders with BitsAndBytes quantization now cast inputs to the actual compute dtype instead of incorrectly casting to integer storage dtypes (uint8/int8).
quote: Fix dtype casting for quantized vision/audio embedders (#46933) by @sharmax-vikas in #46933

from_fact: inputs incorrectly cast to integer storage dtypes (uint8/int8)
inputs cast to actual compute dtype

subject: Gemma4 vision pooler
old: float16 inputs scaled directly
new: inputs cast to float32 before scaling
truth: Gemma4 vision pooler now casts inputs to float32 before scaling, which may cause minor numerical differences for users running Gemma-4 vision models in float16.
quote: The Gemma4 vision pooler now casts inputs to float32 before scaling to prevent float16 overflow (inf saturation) with large checkpoints, which may cause minor numerical differences in outputs for users running Gemma-4 vision models in float16.

from_fact: inputs cast to float32 before scaling

quote: Audio Language Models (ALMs) now have a dedicated base model class without a language modeling head, aligning them with the design of Vision Language Models (VLMs); users relying on the previous model class structure should update their code to use the new base model class where appropriate.

alms now have a dedicated base model class without a language modeling head

old: Apex integration (including RMSNorm usage in T5 and related models)

quote: Apex integration has been removed from the library (including RMSNorm usage in T5 and related models), so users relying on Apex for mixed precision or fused ops should migrate to PyTorch's native equivalents instead.

from_fact: apex integration (including rmsnorm usage in t5 and related models)

subject: AutoTokenizer
old: AutoTokenizer initializes the correct tokenizer class for models like DeepSeek R1
new: AutoTokenizer may initialize the wrong tokenizer class for models like DeepSeek R1
truth: AutoTokenizer has a bug that causes the wrong tokenizer class to be initialized, causing regressions in models like DeepSeek R1.
quote: There was a bug in AutoTokenizer that caused the wrong tokenizer class to be initialized. This caused regressions in models like DeepSeek R1.

from_fact: autotokenizer initializes the correct tokenizer class for models like deepseek r1
autotokenizer may initialize the wrong tokenizer class for models like deepseek r1

subject: num_return_sequences warning on continuous batching path
old: A warning about num_return_sequences being unsupported was shown on the continuous batching path even when the feature worked correctly.
truth: Stop relying on the warning about num_return_sequences on the continuous batching path; it has been removed.
quote: removing misleading warnings about `num_return_sequences` and other unsupported features that were incorrectly firing even when functionality worked correctly

from_fact: a warning about num_return_sequences being unsupported was shown on the continuous batching path even when the feature worked correctly.

subject: misleading warnings about unsupported features on continuous batching path
old: Warnings about unsupported features (other than num_return_sequences) were shown on the continuous batching path even when the features worked correctly.
truth: Stop relying on warnings about unsupported features on the continuous batching path; they have been removed.
quote: removing misleading warnings about `num_return_sequences` and other unsupported features that were incorrectly firing even when functionality worked correctly

from_fact: warnings about unsupported features (other than num_return_sequences) were shown on the continuous batching path even when the features worked correctly.

subject: PreTrainedTokenizerFast
old: BPE tokenizers had clean_up_tokenization applied
new: BPE tokenizers skip clean_up_tokenization
truth: Stop relying on clean_up_tokenization being applied for BPE tokenizers in PreTrainedTokenizerFast; it is now skipped.
quote: fix: skip `clean_up_tokenization` for BPE tokenizers in `PreTrainedTokenizerFast`

subject: rotary_fn in Attention module
old: self.rotary_fn(...) registered as hidden kernel function
truth: Stop using self.rotary_fn(...) in Attention modules; call the rotary function directly instead.
quote: The internal `rotary_fn` is no longer registered as a hidden kernel function, so any code referencing `self.rotary_fn(...)` within an Attention module will break and must be updated to call the function directly instead.

from_fact: self.rotary_fn(...) registered as hidden kernel function

subject: Qwen2.5-VL temporal RoPE scaling
old: temporal RoPE scaling was applied to still images
new: temporal RoPE scaling is no longer applied to still images
truth: Stop relying on temporal RoPE scaling being applied to still images in Qwen2.5-VL; it was a bug and has been fixed.
quote: Fix Qwen2.5-VL temporal RoPE scaling applied to still images

from_fact: temporal rope scaling was applied to still images
temporal rope scaling is no longer applied to still images

subject: Expert Parallelism (EP) shape handling
old: RouterParallel shape, tp_plan property, grouped_mm sentinels could produce silently wrong results or NaN loss
new: these EP bugs are fixed, changing the numerical behaviour of EP training
truth: Stop relying on the previous (buggy) numerical behaviour of Expert Parallelism; RouterParallel shape, tp_plan property, and grouped_mm sentinels have been fixed and may produce different results.
quote: Fix EP: RouterParallel shape, tp_plan property, grouped_mm sentinels

from_fact: routerparallel shape, tp_plan property, grouped_mm sentinels could produce silently wrong results or nan loss
these ep bugs are fixed, changing the numerical behaviour of ep training

subject: FSDP non-rank-0 weight handling
old: NaN weights could appear on non-rank-0 FSDP processes
new: NaN weights on non-rank-0 FSDP processes are fixed
truth: Stop working around NaN weights on non-rank-0 FSDP processes; the bug has been fixed.
quote: Fix NaN weights on non-rank-0 FSDP processes

from_fact: nan weights could appear on non-rank-0 fsdp processes
nan weights on non-rank-0 fsdp processes are fixed

subject: PP-DocLayoutV3 resize
old: resize could fail due to zero-sized masks
new: resize no longer fails on zero-sized masks
truth: Stop working around resize failures caused by zero-sized masks in PP-DocLayoutV3; the bug is fixed.
quote: Fix resize failure caused by zero-sized masks in PP-DocLayoutV3

from_fact: resize could fail due to zero-sized masks
resize no longer fails on zero-sized masks

subject: Kimi-K2.5 tokenizer
old: Kimi-K2.5 tokenizer had a regression and _patch_mistral_regex AttributeError
new: Kimi-K2.5 tokenizer regression and _patch_mistral_regex AttributeError are fixed

kimi-k2.5 tokenizer regression and _patch_mistral_regex attributeerror are fixed

old: streaming generation crashed due to incorrect _tokenizer attribute access
new: streaming generation no longer crashes from _tokenizer attribute access
truth: Stop working around streaming generation crashes in Qwen3VLProcessor caused by incorrect _tokenizer attribute access; the bug is fixed.
quote: fix(serving): resolve rust tokenizer from ProcessorMixin in streaming generation

from_fact: streaming generation crashed due to incorrect _tokenizer attribute access

subject: GPT-SW3 instruct tokenizer
old: GPT-SW3 instruct tokenizer was available in the main repo
truth: Stop using the GPT-SW3 instruct tokenizer from the main transformers repo; it has been moved to an internal testing repo.
quote: Move gpt sw3 tokenizer out

from_fact: gpt-sw3 instruct tokenizer was available in the main repo

subject: Gemma4/Gemma3n KV state sharing
old: KV state sharing was tied to the Cache class
new: KV state sharing is dissociated from the Cache class and always happens regardless of Cache usage
truth: Stop relying on KV state sharing being conditional on Cache usage in Gemma4 and Gemma3n; KV states are now always shared.
quote: Dissociate kv states sharing from the Cache

from_fact: kv state sharing was tied to the cache class
kv state sharing is dissociated from the cache class and always happens regardless of cache usage

subject: text-to-speech pipeline
old: text-to-speech pipeline could crash when generation config contains None values
new: text-to-speech pipeline no longer crashes when generation config contains None values
truth: The text-to-speech pipeline previously crashed when generation configs contained None values; this crash has been fixed, so code relying on that crash behavior (e.g., catching exceptions) will silently stop catching.
quote: Fix `text-to-speech` pipeline crash when generation config contains `None` values (#45107)

from_fact: text-to-speech pipeline could crash when generation config contains none values
text-to-speech pipeline no longer crashes when generation config contains none values

subject: MoE routers
old: MoE routers returning probabilities
new: MoE routers returning logits
truth: MoE routers now return logits instead of probabilities; code that treats router outputs as probabilities (e.g. applying softmax again or interpreting them as probabilities) will silently misbehave.
quote: Fix MoE routers returning probabilities instead of logits (#45131)

subject: Gemma4 shared weights
old: shared weights being loaded/expected
new: all shared weights removed and silently skipped during loading
truth: Gemma4 models no longer have shared weights; any code relying on shared weight tensors being present or loaded will break or silently get None.
quote: [gemma4] Remove all shared weights, and silently skip them during loading (#45336)

from_fact: all shared weights removed and silently skipped during loading

subject: from_pretrained with conversion required
old: silently loading or skipping conversion
new: throws error when conversion is required
truth: from_pretrained now throws an error when conversion is required instead of proceeding silently; code that relied on automatic conversion will break.
quote: throw error when conversion required (#45078)

from_fact: throws error when conversion is required

subject: Dots1MoE override
old: Dots1MoE had its own override
new: Dots1MoE override dropped to `pass` (inherits from DSV3 MoE)
truth: Dots1MoE no longer has its own MoE override and now inherits from DeepseekV3 MoE; any code depending on Dots1MoE-specific MoE behaviour will silently get different behaviour.
quote: refactor(Dots1): drop Dots1MoE override to `pass` (inherits from DSV3 MoE) (#45572)

from_fact: dots1moe override dropped to `pass` (inherits from dsv3 moe)

subject: Zamba2MambaMixer use_mamba_kernels=False
old: use_mamba_kernels=False was ignored
new: use_mamba_kernels=False is now respected
truth: Zamba2MambaMixer previously ignored use_mamba_kernels=False; now it correctly disables mamba kernels when set to False, which may break code that relied on the old (always-on) behaviour.
quote: Fix Zamba2MambaMixer ignoring use_mamba_kernels=False (#44853)

subject: Gemma4 device map auto
old: device map auto behaviour
new: fixed device map auto
truth: Gemma4 device map auto behaviour has been fixed; code relying on the previous (broken) device mapping may break or behave differently.
quote: [gemma4] Fix device map auto (#45347)

subject: Gemma4 gradient accumulation loss and last token incorrect labels
old: incorrect labels for last token during gradient accumulation
new: fixed gradient accumulation loss and last token labels
truth: Gemma4 gradient accumulation loss and last token labels were incorrect and have been fixed; training code that worked around the bug may now break or produce different loss values.
quote: fix gemma4 gradient accumulation loss and last token incorrect labels (#45354)

from_fact: incorrect labels for last token during gradient accumulation
fixed gradient accumulation loss and last token labels

subject: Wav2Vec2Config.vocab_size type
old: vocab_size did not allow None
new: vocab_size now allows None

subject: SmolVLM video processor resize interpolation
old: wrong interpolation after backend refactor
new: fixed interpolation

subject: TF32 being enabled in testing
old: TF32 unexpectedly enabled
new: TF32 no longer unexpectedly enabled

subject: torch.backends.cudnn.conv.fp32_precision
old: not explicitly set
new: explicitly set

old: AttributeError

old: incorrect temporal grid positions
new: fixed temporal grid positions

old: crashed on tool_call messages without content

subject: Gemma4 image preprocessing
old: Standard ImageNet mean/std normalization applied to images
new: No standard ImageNet mean/std normalization; the model's patch embedding layer handles scaling internally (shifts values to [-1, 1])
truth: Stop applying ImageNet mean/std normalization to images for Gemma4; the patch embedding layer handles scaling internally.
quote: Gemma 4 does **not** apply the standard ImageNet mean/std normalization that many other vision models use. The model's own patch embedding layer handles the final scaling internally (shifting values to the [-1, 1] range).

from_fact: standard imagenet mean/std normalization applied to images
no standard imagenet mean/std normalization; the model's patch embedding layer handles scaling internally (shifts values to [-1, 1])

subject: Mamba/hybrid model caches
old: previous workarounds for Mamba-based or hybrid (Mamba + attention) model caches
new: new native cache classes
truth: Stop using previous workarounds for Mamba and hybrid model caches; use the new native cache classes instead.
quote: Mamba and hybrid model caches are now first-class native citizens in the library, so users working with Mamba-based or hybrid (Mamba + attention) models should update their code to use the new native cache classes instead of any previous workarounds.

from_fact: previous workarounds for mamba-based or hybrid (mamba + attention) model caches

subject: LightGlue remote code execution
old: loading LightGlue with trust_remote_code=True
truth: Stop using trust_remote_code=True with LightGlue; use the model through the standard native API instead.
quote: Remote code execution support has been removed from the native LightGlue integration, so users who were loading LightGlue with trust_remote_code=True must remove that argument and use the model directly through the standard native API.

new: Gemma vision mask now supports video inputs

gemma vision mask now supports video inputs

old: Janus model image generation had a bug

old: Local code resolution for tokenizers and image processors was broken
new: Local code resolution for tokenizers and image processors is fixed

subject: AutoConfig.from_pretrained
old: remote code config was preferred over registered config
new: registered config is now preferred over remote code
truth: AutoConfig.from_pretrained now prefers registered config over remote code when both exist, which may silently change which config class is loaded.
